topPlaces.name: Uvari
topPlaces.description: Uvari is a coastal village in Thisayanvilai Taluk, Tirunelveli district, Tamil Nadu, India. The total geographical area of the Uvari is around 3 km2 (1.2 sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Idaiyangudi
topPlaces.description: Idaiyangudi is a village, at Idaiyangudi panchayat in Tirunelveli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It lies between Uvari and Thisayanvilai.

topPlaces.name: Kalikumarapuram
topPlaces.description: Kalikumarapuram is a village situated on Tiruchendur on the Kanyakumari East Coast Road in the taluk of Thisayanvilai, Tirunelveli district, Tamil Nadu. Around 450 people live there.

topPlaces.name: Thisayanvilai
topPlaces.description: Thisayanvilai is a special grade-town Panchayat. It is one of the taluks in Tirunelveli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Manal Matha Shrine
topPlaces.description: Athisaya Manal Matha Shrine (Tamil: அதிசய மணல் மாதா, lit. 'Our Lady of Red Sands'code: tam promoted to code: ta ) is a shrine in southern Thoothukudi district of Tamil Nadu. The shrine belongs to the Sokkan Kudierappu Parish of the Diocese of Tuticorin.

topPlaces.name: Periyathalai
topPlaces.description: Periyathalai is a coastal hamlet located in the southern side of Tuticorin district in Tamil Nadu, India. It is about 53 km from Tirunelveli city.

items.note: Uvari is a coastal village in Thisayanvilai Taluk, Tirunelveli district, Tamil Nadu, India. The total geographical area of the Uvari is around 3 km2 (1.2 sq mi).
Idaiyangudi is a village, at Idaiyangudi panchayat in Tirunelveli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It lies between Uvari and Thisayanvilai.
Kalikumarapuram is a village situated on Tiruchendur on the Kanyakumari East Coast Road in the taluk of Thisayanvilai, Tirunelveli district, Tamil Nadu. Around 450 people live there.
Thisayanvilai is a special grade-town Panchayat. It is one of the taluks in Tirunelveli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

Athisaya Manal Matha Shrine (Tamil: அதிசய மணல் மாதா, lit. 'Our Lady of Red Sands'code: tam promoted to code: ta ) is a shrine in southern Thoothukudi district of Tamil Nadu. The shrine belongs to the Sokkan Kudierappu Parish of the Diocese of Tuticorin.
Periyathalai is a coastal hamlet located in the southern side of Tuticorin district in Tamil Nadu, India. It is about 53 km from Tirunelveli city.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Suyambulingaswamy Temple, Uvari?
faq.a: The best time to visit Suyambulingaswamy Temple, Uvari is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Suyambulingaswamy Temple, Uvari?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (110 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (107 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Suyambulingaswamy Temple, Uvari?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Suyambulingaswamy Temple, Uvari at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Suyambulingaswamy Temple, Uvari?
faq.a: Stays in Suyambulingaswamy Temple, Uvari start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Suyambulingaswamy Temple, Uvari?
faq.a: The top attractions are Uvari, Idaiyangudi, Kalikumarapuram, Thisayanvilai, Manal Matha Shrine.
